response.justification: Bogdan is a strong full-stack and distributed systems engineer with impressive pedigree (Google, Meta) and genuine high-throughput infrastructure experience at Dunnhumby, but he is fundamentally mismatched to this role's core requirements. The job mandates 8 years of C++ experience; the resume shows roughly 6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments plus a thesis project, with C++ absent from the skills section entirely. The role also demands 5 years in large-scale cloud compute or hardware-adjacent infrastructure, whereas his infrastructure work is primarily application-layer (TypeScript, Node.js, AWS CDK) with cloud infrastructure as a secondary concern. His leadership experience is present but implicit and community-oriented rather than formal technical-lead ownership of a platform org.
strengths: Proven high-throughput distributed systems experience: low-latency micro-services handling millions of HTTP req/sec at Dunnhumby demonstrates real scale
Strong testing and engineering-quality culture: TDD advocacy, CI/CD, trunk-based dev, and authored open-source testing frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial)
Staff-level positions at Google and Meta signal seniority and ability to operate in large, complex engineering organizations
concerns: C++ experience is critically insufficient: the role requires 8 years; the resume shows ~6 months at NI plus a student thesis — a near-total mismatch on the primary technical requirement

No cloud compute platform or hardware-architecture depth: infrastructure work is application-layer (AWS CDK for pricing pipelines, Kubernetes for a side project) rather than the compute/storage/network layer the role targets

Formal technical leadership is absent: Staff Engineer titles and community workshops do not substitute for documented experience owning platform strategy, setting architectural standards for a team, or mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers in a line-leadership capacity
